title: Best spots in Tokyo to buy Evangelion merchandise
display_title: Best spots in Tokyo to buy Evangelion merchandise
wikitext:
[]: Best spots in Tokyo to buy Evangelion merchandise?             Quote Post by yecherubs » Wed Jul 28, 2010 9:35 pm Am planning a trip to Tokyo in the next few weeks and would like to visit some Evangelion-only merchandise stores and concept stores if possible, do such stores exist in Tokyo? If not, do ppl know where the Evangelion merchandise hotspots are? Otherwise, any other must-visit sites? I read about the Hakone Lawson (which unfortunately closed down) as well as the Evangelion theme park (though not sure where that is). Any help very much appreciated!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jul 28, 2010 11:35 pm I think I remember mention of stores taking one month or so to sell only Eva products after 2.0 came out in theaters, but that ended in like a week because of the otaku hoards. Check out Akiharbara if you want to get some good shopping done; one of the must-see places if you're going to be in Tokyo. When I was there a few years ago, I went there for a day and literally wandered from store to store checking out all the merch. IIRC, there was plenty of Eva stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Jul 29, 2010 4:21 pm Akihabara is especially good for new stuff. However, if you want to look at picking up some cool older goods, used books, production materials, etc., go to the Mandarake superstore near Nakano station. Google should give you some pointers. They're so big that they keep English-speaking staff on hand.

[]:             Quote Post by yecherubs » Thu Jul 29, 2010 11:02 pm Sachi and drinian, thanks so much for the helpful pointers, I'll definitely check these places out Noticed that there is an official store called www.evastore.jp which seems to be reopening on Aug 10, so will plan my trip to go then Anyone else seen this store before?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Jul 30, 2010 5:57 am That's an online store... °3° But if you're in Japan when it re-opens, you can buy stuff and have it shipped to you (Evastore doesn't ship overseas).

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Sun Aug 01, 2010 5:18 pm When you go to Akihabara, make sure you don't mess with Otaku's

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Aug 04, 2010 8:09 pm KLVTZ wrote: When you go to Akihabara, make sure you don't mess with Otaku's

[]:             Quote Post by Voland » Fri Aug 06, 2010 2:46 pm KLVTZ wrote: When you go to Akihabara, make sure you don't mess with Otaku's

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Aug 06, 2010 6:14 pm Voland's right! Truth is, I'm an Otaku fan and anyone who messes with Evangelion (or Dragon Ball Z) will be messed up badly.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Sat Aug 07, 2010 4:22 pm tomrule123 wrote: Voland's right! Truth is, I'm an Otaku fan and anyone who messes with Evangelion (or Dragon Ball Z) will be messed up badly.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Aug 07, 2010 8:58 pm KLVTZ wrote: i think you indirectly backed me up and not voland lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Aug 11, 2010 12:11 pm Tokyu Hands in Ikebukuro is where I bought all my Eva stuff. It's by the Prince Hotel across from a Sega Arcade and Animate (and under the bridge). The forth floor has a small section of Eva stuff, but it is all the stuff you can usually only find on the Eva online Store. That is where I got my Rei drinking glass and Lilith dinner plate, and some cool NERV logo metal stickers for my phone, as well as your typical shirts and glasses. Not many figures, but you can get those anywhere in Japan. If you want cool nicknacks go to Tokyu Hands.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Sep 05, 2010 8:42 am There's also a big Tokyu Hands in Shinjuku, but I've only ever gone there for DIY and stationery stuff, so I wasn't paying attention to the figure selection. EDIT: Yesterday, I went to the Don Quijote in Roppongi, and I noticed that they have a pretty big selection of odd Eva stuff, mostly mobile phone or computer-related. EDIT EDIT: I have held the NERV bucket in my hands. It, along with a lot of other odd 2.0 merchandise (like canned bread), can be found at the Tokyo Anime Center near Akihabara station. It's also good for Macross curry.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 11:09 am drinian wrote: I have held the NERV bucket in my hands.
